title: FIFA and AFC enable Afghan women to represent their country in official matches through landmark governance reform
summary: The FIFA Council today approved a momentous amendment to the FIFA Governance Regulations, which enables Afghan female players – including members of Afghan Women United – to represent their country in official international matches as part of FIFA.
full_text: The FIFA Council today approved a momentous amendment to the FIFA Governance Regulations, which enables Afghan female players – including members of Afghan Women United – to represent their country in official international matches as part of FIFA.
why: FIFA and AFC enable Afghan women to represent their country in official matches.
headline: Afghan Women Break Barriers in FIFA Governance Reform

title: FIFA Council increases record financial distribution to all 48 Participating Member Associations at the FIFA World Cup 2026™
summary: The FIFA Council met ahead of the 76th FIFA Congress in Vancouver, Canada, a Host City for the FIFA World Cup 2026™, 44 days before the kick-off of the tournament.
full_text: The FIFA Council met ahead of the 76th FIFA Congress in Vancouver, Canada, a Host City for the FIFA World Cup 2026™, 44 days before the kick-off of the tournament.

headline: Record FIFA World Cup 2026 financial boost for all 48 teams

title: FIFA Series™ provides a launchpad for Mauritanian women’s football
summary: After playing at the FIFA Series 2026™ in Côte d’Ivoire, Mauritania will enter the FIFA/Coca-Cola Women’s World Ranking for the first time.

title: FIFA extends temporary rules due to ongoing war in Ukraine
summary: In view of the ongoing war in Ukraine, the Bureau of the FIFA Council has decided today to extend the application of Annexe 7 of the Regulations on the Status and Transfer of Players (RSTP) until 30 June 2026.
full_text: In view of the ongoing war in Ukraine, the Bureau of the FIFA Council has decided today to extend the application of Annexe 7 of the Regulations on the Status and Transfer of Players (RSTP) until 30 June 2026.
why: FIFA extends temporary rules due to ongoing war in Ukraine
headline: FIFA Extends Temporary Rules Amid Ongoing Ukraine War
ai_summary: ফিফা কাউন্সিলটি রস্টিপ নিয়মাবলীর অ্যানেক্স ৭ এর পরিসর শেষ পর্যন্ত ৩০ জুন ২০২৬ পর্যন্ত বাড়িয়েছে, যুক্তরাষ্ট্রে চলমান সংঘাতের কারণে বলে উল্লেখ করেছে। এই ধারণাটি হল যে এটি যুক্রেনীয় ফুটবল খেলোয়াড়দের জন্য সম্পৃক্ত এই সময়টিতে স্থিতিশীলতা এবং সহায়তা প্রদান করার লক্ষ্যে।
facebook_caption: In a move to support Ukrainian footballers amidst the ongoing war, the FIFA Council has decided to extend the temporary rules until 30 June 2026. This decision aims to provide stability and protection to players from Ukraine. The Bureau of the FIFA Council made this announcement today, emphasizing the need for continued support during this challenging time.

title: FIFA and FIFPRO sign landmark agreement to usher in new era of collaboration and player representation in global football governance
summary: On the eve of the FIFA World Cup 2026™, FIFA and FIFPRO have concluded a historic Memorandum of Understanding (MoU), opening a new chapter in cooperation between the two organisations and football governance in general.
full_text: On the eve of the FIFA World Cup 2026™, FIFA and FIFPRO have concluded a historic Memorandum of Understanding (MoU), opening a new chapter in cooperation between the two organisations and football governance in general.
why: FIFA and FIFPRO have concluded a historic Memorandum of Understanding, ushering in a new era of collaboration and player representation in global football governance.
headline: FIFA, FIFPRO Unite for New Era in Football Governance

title: FIFA Series 2026™ helps Thailand promote women’s football
summary: This year’s FIFA Series 2026™ marks an important milestone for Football Association of Thailand; it will be the first FIFA competition since the FIFA Futsal World Cup 2012 was held in the Southeast Asian nation.
full_text: This year’s FIFA Series 2026™ marks an important milestone for Football Association of Thailand; it will be the first FIFA competition since the FIFA Futsal World Cup 2012 was held in the Southeast Asian nation.

headline: Thailand Returns to FIFA Competition After 14 Years
